_status: partially populated (2026-07-07 second + third research pass) - see data-requirements.md §17/§18 and ISSUES.md ISS-9/ISS-3

usefulLifeYears.note: Companies Act Schedule II names 'Cat-scan and ultrasound machines' as the diagnostic-scan category (13 years) -- MRI is grouped here as the same class of diagnostic imaging equipment. See data-requirements.md §12.4/§14.

salvageValuePercentage.note: Companies Act Schedule II caps residual value at 5% of original cost unless separately justified; no evidence found of a different treatment for medical equipment. Citation corrected to S8 (Schedule II itself, already used for usefulLifeYears) -- the third research pass cited an Income Tax depreciation-rates page, which looks like a citation mismatch (different regime). Full independent verification of the exact Schedule II clause was not completed -- see data-requirements.md §18.2.

installationAndAncillaryCostPercentage.note: NEIGRIHMS tenders require a minimum 10% of product cost allocated to installation/commissioning/testing; a Borooah Cancer Institute MRI tender sets the minimum at 30%. These are tender-mandated bid-allocation floors, not measured actual average spend -- see data-requirements.md §18.3.

warrantyYears.note: Two independent government tenders (Shirdi Saibaba Sansthan Trust 3T MRI; NEIGRIHMS 1.5T MRI) both specify 5-year comprehensive warranty. See data-requirements.md §18.1.

cmcYears.note: Follows the 5-year warranty in the NEIGRIHMS tender. See data-requirements.md §18.4.

amcAnnualCostPercentage.unit: percent of equipment cost per year
amcAnnualCostPercentage.note: Labour-only AMC. This is a single generic tender clause (SCTIMST) applied identically across all 5 equipment types in the absence of equipment-specific AMC data -- not independently researched for MRI specifically. See data-requirements.md §18.4.

cmcAnnualCostPercentage.unit: percent of equipment cost per year
cmcAnnualCostPercentage.note: IMPORTANT CONTRADICTION -- do not use as sole default: this is a generic tender-ceiling range (what vendors are contractually allowed to bid up to), not equipment-specific. A peer-reviewed life-cycle-costing study of one real AIIMS 1.5T MRI (S53) found ACTUAL post-warranty CMC cost was only ~0.23-0.28% of equipment value per year (Rs 9.7 lakh over 5 years on a ~Rs 7.5 crore machine) -- roughly 25-30x lower than this range. The tender range and the real-cost study may be measuring genuinely different things (contractual ceiling vs. one hospital's negotiated/actual experience), or one could be anomalous. Needs a human decision before either number becomes a UI default -- see data-requirements.md §18.4.

_observedActualAlternative.unit: percent of equipment cost per year
_observedActualAlternative.note: One AIIMS hospital's real observed cost from a peer-reviewed life-cycle-costing study. May be unusually low (negotiated government rate) rather than representative of a typical private hospital.

_bedVolumeTierHypothesis.status: hypothesis, not yet researched -- do not use for defaults
_bedVolumeTierHypothesis.hypothesis: AIIMS is a very large, very high-volume referral institute (order of 2000+ beds by public reputation, not independently verified/cited this session), which plausibly negotiates CMC/AMC contract rates far below the generic tender-ceiling range that smaller-volume private hospitals would actually pay. If true, the S51/S52 vs S53 contradiction isn't really a contradiction -- it's two different points on a volume/bed-count curve, not two competing estimates of the same thing.
_bedVolumeTierHypothesis.proposedTierStructure: Reuse data-requirements.md §2.3's existing bed-size buckets (<50, 50-100, 101-250, 251-500, >500) rather than inventing a new bucketing scheme, but split the open-ended ">500" bucket further (e.g. 501-1000, >1000) since a large referral institute like AIIMS is a materially different tier than a 600-bed private hospital -- both currently fall in the same bucket.
_bedVolumeTierHypothesis.nextAction: See data-requirements.md §19 -- needs (a) independent verification of AIIMS's actual bed count before treating the volume theory as confirmed, and (b) a targeted research pass looking for CMC/AMC (and other volume-sensitive contract terms, e.g. consumable/reagent supply deals) broken out by hospital bed-count or scan-volume tier, not just a single blended figure per equipment type.

_usagePerDayRange.note: typical=23 is a real single-hospital Indian study (NIMS, tertiary teaching hospital -- a busy referral centre, may run higher than a typical smaller private setup). low/high=20/40 is global context (not India-specific).

billedTariffPerUse.note: MRI head/brain (plain) CGHS reimbursement ceiling -- a government-scheme floor, NOT a private cash tariff (private hospitals typically charge more). Other body parts price differently (e.g. MRI knee ~2380/2800, MRI head with contrast ~4250/5000) -- this field anchors to the plain head/brain baseline only. A stale (2013) private-hospital data point exists for context (S31): ~INR 2500, 13 years old, do not use as a current default.

launchDelayMonths.note: Converted from 6-10 weeks (civil work, RF shielding, magnet commissioning). Single vendor/supplier blog, not empirical. MRI does not require AERB licensing (non-ionizing).
